commands.review_summary:
This PR guards `normalizeOrigins()` against opaque parsed origins and adds a util regression test for `file://` and `about:blank` inputs.

Reproducibility: yes. I reproduced current-main behavior with the checked-in dist: `getCookies()` throws `TypeError: Invalid URL` for the `file://` inline payload path, and Node's URL API confirms the opaque origin is `null`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can keep the PR's no-throw behavior while adding public API coverage and preventing empty-origin inline reads from returning unrelated cookies.

target.title: fix(core): drop opaque origins so getCookies doesn't throw on file:// URLs
target.body:
### Bug

`normalizeOrigins()` pushes `parsed.origin` without checking for opaque origins. For `file://`, `about:blank`, `data:`, etc. the WHATWG URL spec defines `origin` as the literal string `"null"`, so `"null/"` ends up in the origins list:

```ts
normalizeOrigins("file:///etc/hosts"); // ["null/"]
```

Every provider then derives hosts with `origins.map((o) => new URL(o).hostname)` (inline.ts, firefoxSqlite.ts, chromeSqlite/shared.ts, safariBinaryCookies.ts), and `new URL("null/")` throws `TypeError: Invalid URL`. None of those call sites are wrapped in try/catch, so `getCookies()` throws instead of returning a best-effort empty result.

### Fix

Skip origins whose value is `"null"`, in both the primary-URL and extra-origins branches. This is consistent with the existing `try/catch` blocks that already ignore malformed inputs (and the documented contract: best-effort results filtered by `url`/`origins`). Two-line change.

### Proof (real behavior, public API)

Built `@steipete/sweet-cookie/core` and called the real `getCookies` with a `file://` URL and an inline cookie payload (so it reaches the inline provider's `origins.map(... new URL ...)`):

```
// BEFORE (main)
$ node -e "import('./dist/index.js').then(m => m.getCookies({url:'file:///etc/hosts', inlineCookiesJson:'{\"cookies\":[{\"name\":\"sid\",\"domain\":\"example.com\"}]}'}))"
THREW: TypeError: Invalid URL

// AFTER (this branch)
RESULT (no throw): {"cookies":1,"warnings":[]}
```

### Test

Added a regression test to `packages/core/tests/util.test.ts` asserting opaque origins are dropped (primary URL and extra origins).

- Before the fix it fails on the new case.
- After the fix: full suite `111 passed | 16 skipped`. `pnpm check` (oxfmt + tsgo typecheck + oxlint --type-aware) clean.

Found and fixed with the help of the Claude CLI.
target.author: devyrpauli
